examplesJson: [{"japanese":"緊急の場合は、このボタンを押してください。","reading":"きんきゅうのばあいは、このボタンをおしてください。","english":"In case of emergency, press this button."},{"japanese":"参加できない場合は、ご連絡ください。","reading":"さんかできないばあいは、ごれんらくください。","english":"If you cannot participate, please contact us."},{"japanese":"天気が悪い場合は、イベントを中止します。","reading":"てんきがわるいばあいは、イベントをちゅうしします。","english":"In the event of bad weather, we will cancel the event."}]

title: 〜たびに (every time / whenever)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + たびに / N の + たびに
meaning: Every time / whenever — marks a recurring situation and its consistent result
explanation: たびに means something happens every single time the first condition occurs. It emphasises regularity and often a consistent emotional or physical reaction. Different from 〜と (which states automatic consequences).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"この歌を聴くたびに、故郷を思い出します。","reading":"このうたをきくたびに、こきょうをおもいだします。","english":"Every time I hear this song, I'm reminded of my hometown."},{"japanese":"会うたびに、背が高くなっていますね。","reading":"あうたびに、せがたかくなっていますね。","english":"Every time we meet, you've gotten taller."},{"japanese":"旅行のたびに、お土産を買います。","reading":"りょこうのたびに、おみやげをかいます。","english":"Every time I travel, I buy souvenirs."}]

title: 〜ことから (from the fact that)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + ことから
meaning: From the fact that / because of — draws a conclusion or derives a name/nickname from evidence
explanation: ことから states the basis or reason from which a conclusion, name, or deduction arises. Often used in explanations of why something is called what it is, or in reasoning chains.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"桜の花が美しいことから、日本の国花とされています。","reading":"さくらのはながうつくしいことから、にほんのこっかとされています。","english":"From the fact that cherry blossoms are beautiful, they are considered Japan's national flower."},{"japanese":"彼が遅刻ばかりすることから、時間を守らない人だと思われています。","reading":"かれがちこくばかりすることから、じかんをまもらないひとだとおもわれています。","english":"From the fact that he's always late, he is thought of as someone who doesn't keep time."},{"japanese":"その川が青く見えることから、「青川」と呼ばれています。","reading":"そのかわがあおくみえることから、「あおかわ」とよばれています。","english":"Because the river looks blue, it is called 'Blue River'."}]

title: 〜ことで (by doing / through)
pattern: V (plain) + ことで
meaning: By doing / through — marks the means or method that leads to the result
explanation: ことで nominalises the verb phrase and marks it as the method or means. Similar to によって but more action-focused. Often used in set phrases or formal explanations of how something is achieved.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"毎日練習することで、技術が上がります。","reading":"まいにちれんしゅうすることで、ぎじゅつがあがります。","english":"By practicing every day, your skills improve."},{"japanese":"対話することで、誤解が解けました。","reading":"たいわすることで、ごかいがとけました。","english":"Through dialogue, the misunderstanding was resolved."},{"japanese":"食事を改善することで、健康になりました。","reading":"しょくじをかいぜんすることで、けんこうになりました。","english":"By improving my diet, I became healthier."}]

title: 〜のに (in order to — purpose with effort)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + のに + required resource/effort
meaning: To do / for doing — marks what is needed or required for a purpose
explanation: This のに (different from the concessive のに) expresses what is necessary or used for a purpose. Similar to ために but more focused on requirements or suitability: このカードはお金を引き出すのに使います.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"この薬は頭痛を治すのに効果があります。","reading":"このくすりはずつうをなおすのにこうかがあります。","english":"This medicine is effective for treating headaches."},{"japanese":"パソコンはレポートを書くのに必要です。","reading":"パソコンはレポートをかくのにひつようです。","english":"A computer is necessary for writing reports."},{"japanese":"この道具は何に使うのですか。","reading":"このどうぐはなににつかうのですか。","english":"What is this tool used for?"}]

title: 〜ものだから (because — emphatic/excuse)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + ものだから / もんだから (casual)
meaning: Because (emphatic) — gives a reason as an explanation or excuse
explanation: ものだから (and its casual form もんだから) gives a reason with a tone of explanation or justification. The speaker uses it to explain why something happened, often as an excuse. Stronger and more personal than ので.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"電車が遅れたものだから、遅刻してしまいました。","reading":"でんしゃがおくれたものだから、ちこくしてしまいました。","english":"Because the train was delayed, I ended up being late."},{"japanese":"急いでいたもんだから、財布を忘れてしまいました。","reading":"いそいでいたもんだから、さいふをわすれてしまいました。","english":"Because I was in a hurry, I forgot my wallet."},{"japanese":"嬉しかったものだから、つい大声で叫んでしまいました。","reading":"うれしかったものだから、ついおおごえでさけんでしまいました。","english":"Because I was so happy, I ended up shouting out loud."}]

title: 〜ところで (even if — futility)
pattern: V-た + ところで
meaning: Even if I were to do — it would be pointless/futile
explanation: たところで expresses futility: even if the action in the first clause were taken, it would not change anything or achieve the desired result. Different from ても (which is neutral) — たところで emphasises uselessness.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"今更謝ったところで、遅すぎます。","reading":"いまさらあやまったところで、おそすぎます。","english":"Even if I were to apologise now, it's too late."},{"japanese":"一人で反対したところで、何も変わりません。","reading":"ひとりでわはんたいしたところで、なにもかわりません。","english":"Even if I were to oppose it alone, nothing would change."},{"japanese":"聞いたところで、分からないと思います。","reading":"きいたところで、わからないとおもいます。","english":"Even if I asked, I don't think I'd understand."}]

title: 〜ということだ (it means that / I heard that)
pattern: S (plain) + ということだ / ということです
meaning: It means that / I hear that — explains meaning or reports information
explanation: ということだ summarises or restates the meaning of something (in other words, what it means). It is also used to report hearsay formally, similar to そうだ. The casual contraction is ってことだ.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼が来ないということは、会議は中止ということですか。","reading":"かれがこないということは、かいぎはちゅうしということですか。","english":"Does the fact that he's not coming mean the meeting is cancelled?"},{"japanese":"つまり、もう諦めたということですか。","reading":"つまり、もうあきらめたということですか。","english":"In other words, does it mean you've already given up?"},{"japanese":"噂では、来月引っ越すということです。","reading":"うわさでは、らいげつひっこすということです。","english":"I hear (by rumour) that she's moving next month."}]

title: 〜にしたがって (as / in accordance with)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + にしたがって / N + にしたがって
meaning: As / following — parallel progression or acting in accordance with something
explanation: にしたがって has two senses: (1) as X increases/progresses, Y changes proportionally; (2) acting in accordance with rules, orders, or someone's lead. Both involve following or going along with something.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"年をとるにしたがって、体力が落ちていきます。","reading":"としをとるにしたがって、たいりょくがおちていきます。","english":"As one gets older, physical strength declines."},{"japanese":"規則にしたがって行動してください。","reading":"きそくにしたがってこうどうしてください。","english":"Please act in accordance with the rules."},{"japanese":"季節の変化にしたがって、景色も変わります。","reading":"きせつのへんかにしたがって、けしきもかわります。","english":"As the seasons change, the scenery changes too."}]

title: 〜を通して / を通じて (through / throughout)
pattern: N + を通して / を通じて
meaning: Through / throughout / by means of — marks a medium, channel, or entire period
explanation: を通して and を通じて are largely interchangeable. Both mean 'through (a medium or channel)' or 'throughout (a time period)'. を通じて can feel slightly more formal or literary.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"インターネットを通じて、世界中の人と話せます。","reading":"インターネットをつうじて、せかいじゅうのひととはなせます。","english":"Through the internet, I can talk to people around the world."},{"japanese":"一年を通して、観光客が来ます。","reading":"いちねんをとおして、かんこうきゃくがきます。","english":"Tourists come throughout the year."},{"japanese":"この経験を通して、多くのことを学びました。","reading":"このけいけんをとおして、おおくのことをまなびました。","english":"Through this experience, I learned a lot."}]

title: 〜向けに / 向けの (aimed at / for)
pattern: N + 向けに / 向けの + N
meaning: Aimed at / designed for / intended for — marks the target audience or purpose
explanation: 向けに marks the intended recipient or target of something. 向けの modifies a following noun. It is common in product descriptions, publications, and services: 子供向けの本 = a book for children.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"この教材は初心者向けに作られています。","reading":"このきょうざいはしょしんしゃむけにつくられています。","english":"This material was made for beginners."},{"japanese":"外国人観光客向けのサービスを提供しています。","reading":"がいこくじんかんこうきゃくむけのサービスをていきょうしています。","english":"We provide services aimed at foreign tourists."},{"japanese":"子供向けに書かれた本です。","reading":"こどもむけにかかれたほんです。","english":"It is a book written for children."}]

title: 〜次第で (depending on)
pattern: N + 次第で / 次第では
meaning: Depending on — outcome varies based on the preceding factor
explanation: 次第で (or 次第では) expresses that the result depends on the preceding noun. 次第 alone (after a verb stem) means 'as soon as'. The two uses are distinct — context clarifies which applies.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"努力次第で、誰でも成功できます。","reading":"どりょくしだいで、だれでもせいこうできます。","english":"Depending on your effort, anyone can succeed."},{"japanese":"天気次第では、予定を変更するかもしれません。","reading":"てんきしだいでは、よていをへんこうするかもしれません。","english":"Depending on the weather, we might change the plan."},{"japanese":"やり方次第で、結果は大きく変わります。","reading":"やりかたしだいで、けっかはおおきくかわります。","english":"Depending on how you do it, results can vary greatly."}]

title: 〜とともに (together with / as ... so)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + とともに / N + とともに
meaning: Together with / along with / as X, so Y — simultaneous change or accompaniment
explanation: とともに has two uses: (1) accompaniment — together with someone; (2) parallel change — as one thing changes, another changes simultaneously. Similar to につれて but more formal and broader in use.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"時代の変化とともに、言葉も変わっていきます。","reading":"じだいのへんかとともに、ことばもかわっていきます。","english":"Along with changes in the era, language also changes."},{"japanese":"彼は家族とともに外国に移りました。","reading":"かれはかぞくとともにがいこくにうつりました。","english":"He moved abroad together with his family."},{"japanese":"春の訪れとともに、花が咲き始めます。","reading":"はるのおとずれとともに、はながさきはじめます。","english":"Along with the arrival of spring, flowers start to bloom."}]

title: 〜につれて (as ... / in proportion to)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + につれて / N + につれて
meaning: As / in proportion to — one change accompanies or causes another proportionally
explanation: につれて expresses proportional or parallel change: as A changes, B changes correspondingly. Usually describes gradual, natural progression. The subjects of both clauses often differ.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"春になるにつれて、だんだん暖かくなってきます。","reading":"はるになるにつれて、だんだんあたたかくなってきます。","english":"As spring arrives, it gradually gets warmer."},{"japanese":"慣れるにつれて、仕事が楽になりました。","reading":"なれるにつれて、しごとがらくになりました。","english":"As I got used to it, the work became easier."},{"japanese":"年をとるにつれて、好みが変わります。","reading":"としをとるにつれて、このみがかわります。","english":"As you age, your preferences change."}]

title: 〜おそれがある (there is a risk/fear that)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + おそれがある / N の + おそれがある
meaning: There is a risk / fear that — expresses potential negative outcome
explanation: おそれがある is a formal expression indicating a risk or possibility of something negative occurring. Common in news, weather reports, and official warnings. The written form uses 恐れ; more formal than かもしれない for negative events.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"台風が上陸するおそれがあります。","reading":"たいふうがじょうりくするおそれがあります。","english":"There is a risk that the typhoon will make landfall."},{"japanese":"このままでは失敗するおそれがあります。","reading":"このままではしっぱいするおそれがあります。","english":"There is a risk of failure if things continue like this."},{"japanese":"感染のおそれがあるため、マスクをしてください。","reading":"かんせんのおそれがあるため、マスクをしてください。","english":"Please wear a mask as there is a risk of infection."}]

title: 〜がちだ (tend to / prone to)
pattern: V-stem + がちだ / N + がちだ
meaning: Tend to / be prone to — habitual tendency, often negative
explanation: がちだ expresses a tendency or inclination, usually for negative or undesirable things. 病気がち = prone to illness; 忘れがち = tend to forget. It implies this happens more than it should.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"冬は風邪をひきがちです。","reading":"ふゆはかぜをひきがちです。","english":"I tend to catch colds in winter."},{"japanese":"忙しいと食事を抜きがちになります。","reading":"いそがしいとしょくじをぬきがちになります。","english":"When busy, I tend to skip meals."},{"japanese":"最近、曇りがちな天気が続いています。","reading":"さいきん、くもりがちなてんきがつづいています。","english":"Recently we've been having mostly cloudy weather."}]

title: 〜気味だ (slightly / a bit — tendency)
pattern: V-stem / N + 気味だ
meaning: A little / slightly — slight tendency or inclination, often physical or negative
explanation: 気味だ (ぎみ) indicates a slight tendency or slight degree of something, usually physical states or situations. 疲れ気味 = a bit tired; 風邪気味 = coming down with a cold. Less strong than がちだ.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"最近、疲れ気味です。","reading":"さいきん、つかれぎみです。","english":"I've been feeling a bit tired lately."},{"japanese":"今日は風邪気味なので、早めに帰ります。","reading":"きょうはかぜぎみなので、はやめにかえります。","english":"I'm coming down with a cold today, so I'll leave early."},{"japanese":"最近、少し太り気味です。","reading":"さいきん、すこしふとりぎみです。","english":"I've been putting on a little weight lately."}]

title: 〜てこそ (only because / precisely because)
pattern: V-て + こそ
meaning: Only because / precisely because — emphasises the indispensable condition
explanation: てこそ means 'it is only through/because of X that Y is possible'. It stresses that X is the essential condition — without it, Y cannot happen. Carries a strong emphasis or assertion.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"失敗してこそ、本当の成長がある。","reading":"しっぱいしてこそ、ほんとうのせいちょうがある。","english":"It is only through failure that true growth occurs."},{"japanese":"努力してこそ、成功の価値がある。","reading":"どりょくしてこそ、せいこうのかちがある。","english":"Success only has value when achieved through effort."},{"japanese":"健康であってこそ、何でもできる。","reading":"けんこうであってこそ、なんでもできる。","english":"Only when you are healthy can you do anything."}]

title: 〜にしては (for / considering that)
pattern: N / V (plain) + にしては
meaning: For / considering — the result is unexpected given the standard or type
explanation: にしては expresses that something is unexpected given the category or standard it belongs to. It often implies the result is better or worse than typical for that category. Similar to 'for someone like...' or 'considering...'.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"初心者にしては、上手ですね。","reading":"しょしんしゃにしては、じょうずですね。","english":"For a beginner, you're quite good."},{"japanese":"今日は春にしては寒いですね。","reading":"きょうははるにしてはさむいですね。","english":"It's cold today, considering it's spring."},{"japanese":"値段にしては、品質がよくないですね。","reading":"ねだんにしては、ひんしつがよくないですね。","english":"Considering the price, the quality isn't great."}]

title: 〜に限って (only ... / especially when)
pattern: N + に限って
meaning: Especially / just when — ironic: a certain person/thing does something unexpectedly
explanation: に限って expresses that something happens specifically (and usually ironically or unfairly) with/to the marked noun. 私に限って = not me of all people / just when it's me it happens. Often carries a nuance of surprise or complaint.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"私に限ってそんなことはしません。","reading":"わたしにかぎってそんなことはしません。","english":"Someone like me would never do such a thing."},{"japanese":"急いでいる時に限って、電車が遅れる。","reading":"いそいでいるときにかぎって、でんしゃがおくれる。","english":"Just when I'm in a hurry, the train is always late."},{"japanese":"大事な試合に限って、雨が降るんだよな。","reading":"だいじなしあいにかぎって、あめがふるんだよな。","english":"It always rains specifically during the important matches."}]

title: 〜はもちろん / はもとより (of course / not to mention)
pattern: N + はもちろん / はもとより + も
meaning: Of course / let alone / not to mention — X is obvious, and Y is also true
explanation: はもちろん means 'of course X, but also Y'. The first item is taken for granted; the second is the added point. はもとより is the more formal/literary version, often used in formal writing.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"英語はもちろん、フランス語も話せます。","reading":"えいごはもちろん、フランスごもはなせます。","english":"Of course she speaks English, and French too."},{"japanese":"健康はもちろん、安全も大切です。","reading":"けんこうはもちろん、あんぜんもたいせつです。","english":"Of course health is important, but safety is too."},{"japanese":"この本は専門家はもとより、一般の方にも役立ちます。","reading":"このほんはせんもんかはもとより、いっぱんのかたにもやくだちます。","english":"This book is useful not only to experts but also to general readers."}]

title: 〜ついでに (while one is at it / taking the opportunity)
pattern: V (plain non-past / た) + ついでに / N の + ついでに
meaning: While you're at it / taking the opportunity — doing a secondary action alongside the primary one
explanation: ついでに means to take advantage of an opportunity (an already-planned action) to also do something else. The secondary action (after ついでに) is done conveniently alongside the main activity.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"買い物のついでに、郵便局に寄ります。","reading":"かいもののついでに、ゆうびんきょくによります。","english":"While I'm out shopping, I'll stop by the post office."},{"japanese":"図書館に行くついでに、本を返しておきます。","reading":"としょかんにいくついでに、ほんをかえしておきます。","english":"Since I'm going to the library anyway, I'll return the books."},{"japanese":"散歩のついでに、コンビニで買い物しました。","reading":"さんぽのついでに、コンビニでかいものしました。","english":"While I was taking a walk, I stopped at the convenience store."}]

title: 〜さえ (even — extreme case marker)
pattern: N + さえ / V-stem + さえ + する
meaning: Even — marks an extreme or unexpected case
explanation: さえ (without ば) marks an extreme case: 'even X (which you wouldn't expect)'. It highlights a surprising example. Can follow nouns, verb stems, or て-forms. This is distinct from さえ〜ば (sufficient condition).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"子供でさえ知っていることです。","reading":"こどもでさえしっていることです。","english":"Even a child knows this."},{"japanese":"彼は食事さえせずに働いていました。","reading":"かれはしょくじさえせずにはたらいていました。","english":"He was working without even eating."},{"japanese":"挨拶さえしてくれれば、それでいいです。","reading":"あいさつさえしてくれれば、それでいいです。","english":"As long as you at least greet me, that's fine."}]

title: 〜ばよかった (should have / wish I had)
pattern: V-ば + よかった
meaning: I should have / I wish I had — expresses regret about a past action
explanation: ばよかった (or なければよかった for the negative) expresses regret. The speaker wishes they had done (or not done) something differently. The ば-form + よかった is the standard structure for this regret pattern.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"もっと早く起きればよかった。","reading":"もっとはやくおきればよかった。","english":"I should have gotten up earlier."},{"japanese":"あの映画を見なければよかったです。","reading":"あのえいがをみなければよかったです。","english":"I wish I hadn't watched that movie."},{"japanese":"傘を持ってくればよかった。","reading":"かさをもってくればよかった。","english":"I should have brought an umbrella."}]

title: 〜に応じて (in response to / according to)
pattern: N + に応じて / に応じた + N
meaning: In response to / according to — adjusts or varies based on the situation
explanation: に応じて expresses adjustment or response to a given condition. It implies flexibility: the response or outcome changes to match the situation. に応じた modifies a following noun attributively.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"状況に応じて対応を変えてください。","reading":"じょうきょうにおうじてたいおうをかえてください。","english":"Please change your response according to the situation."},{"japanese":"能力に応じた仕事を任せます。","reading":"のうりょくにおうじたしごとをまかせます。","english":"I'll assign work according to ability."},{"japanese":"ニーズに応じてサービスをカスタマイズします。","reading":"ニーズにおうじてサービスをカスタマイズします。","english":"We will customise the service according to your needs."}]

title: 〜とか〜とか (things like / such as)
pattern: N/V (plain) + とか + N/V (plain) + とか
meaning: Things like / such as — non-exhaustive list with casual, approximate nuance
explanation: とか〜とか lists examples in a casual way, implying there are other items too (like や but more colloquial). Can list nouns, verbs, or quoted speech. Sounds more conversational than や〜など.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"週末はゲームをするとか、映画を見るとかします。","reading":"しゅうまつはゲームをするとか、えいがをみるとかします。","english":"On weekends I do things like playing games and watching movies."},{"japanese":"果物とか野菜とかが好きです。","reading":"くだものとかやさいとかがすきです。","english":"I like things like fruits and vegetables."},{"japanese":"「疲れた」とか「もう無理」とか言っていました。","reading":"「つかれた」とか「もうむり」とかいっていました。","english":"She was saying things like 'I'm tired' and 'I can't go on'."}]

title: 〜にもかかわらず (despite / in spite of)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + にもかかわらず / N + にもかかわらず
meaning: Despite / in spite of / regardless of — formal concession
explanation: にもかかわらず is a formal concessive conjunction meaning 'despite' or 'in spite of'. The result is contrary to what the first clause would suggest. More formal than のに or ても.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"雨にもかかわらず、大勢の人が来ました。","reading":"あめにもかかわらず、おおぜいのひとがきました。","english":"Despite the rain, many people came."},{"japanese":"努力したにもかかわらず、失敗しました。","reading":"どりょくしたにもかかわらず、しっぱいしました。","english":"Despite making an effort, I failed."},{"japanese":"注意したにもかかわらず、同じミスを繰り返しました。","reading":"ちゅういしたにもかかわらず、おなじミスをくりかえしました。","english":"Despite being warned, he repeated the same mistake."}]

title: 〜という点で (in terms of the point that)
pattern: S (plain) + という点で / という点では
meaning: In terms of / from the perspective that — evaluation based on a specific criterion
explanation: という点で frames a comparison or evaluation using a specific criterion or aspect. Often used in more analytical or formal speech. 点 (point/aspect) makes the basis of judgement explicit.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"コストという点では、A案のほうが優れています。","reading":"コストというてんでは、Aあんのほうがすぐれています。","english":"In terms of cost, Plan A is superior."},{"japanese":"使いやすさという点で、このアプリは優秀です。","reading":"つかいやすさというてんで、このアプリはゆうしゅうです。","english":"In terms of ease of use, this app is excellent."},{"japanese":"安全という点では問題ありません。","reading":"あんぜんというてんでは問題ありません。","english":"There is no problem in terms of safety."}]

title: 〜に加えて (in addition to)
pattern: N + に加えて / に加え
meaning: In addition to — adds a further element on top of what was already stated
explanation: に加えて introduces an additional factor, item, or quality. Similar to 上に but more neutral in tone (doesn't imply compounding the same direction). に加え is the slightly more formal variant.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"英語に加えて、スペイン語も勉強しています。","reading":"えいごにくわえて、スペインごもべんきょうしています。","english":"In addition to English, I'm also studying Spanish."},{"japanese":"給料に加えて、ボーナスも出ました。","reading":"きゅうりょうにくわえて、ボーナスもでました。","english":"In addition to the salary, a bonus was also given."},{"japanese":"経験に加えて、資格も必要です。","reading":"けいけんにくわえて、しかくもひつようです。","english":"In addition to experience, qualifications are also required."}]

title: 〜のではないか / んじゃないか (I wonder if / don't you think)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + のではないか / んじゃないか
meaning: Don't you think / I wonder if — indirect conjecture or soft assertion
explanation: のではないか presents a conjecture with less force than だろう — it invites the listener's agreement rather than asserting. んじゃないか is the casual form. Often used when the speaker has some evidence but isn't certain.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼はもう知っているのではないかと思います。","reading":"かれはもうしっているのではないかとおもいます。","english":"I wonder if he doesn't already know."},{"japanese":"この方法は間違っているんじゃないですか。","reading":"このほうほうはまちがっているんじゃないですか。","english":"Don't you think this method is wrong?"},{"japanese":"もう少し考えたほうがいいんじゃないでしょうか。","reading":"もうすこしかんがえたほうがいいんじゃないでしょうか。","english":"Don't you think it would be better to think about it a bit more?"}]

title: 〜に関して / に関する (concerning / regarding)
pattern: N + に関して / に関する + N
meaning: Concerning / regarding — marks the subject matter of discussion or inquiry
explanation: に関して is a formal way to mark the topic of a statement, question, or action. に関する precedes a noun (a question regarding...). It is more formal than について and common in academic, business, and official contexts.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"この件に関してご意見をお聞かせください。","reading":"このけんにかんしてごいけんをおきかせください。","english":"Please share your opinion regarding this matter."},{"japanese":"環境問題に関する報告書を読みました。","reading":"かんきょうもんだいにかんするほうこくしょをよみました。","english":"I read a report concerning environmental issues."},{"japanese":"契約に関してご不明な点はありますか。","reading":"けいやくにかんしてごふめいなてんはありますか。","english":"Do you have any unclear points regarding the contract?"}]

title: 〜をもとに (based on)
pattern: N + をもとに / をもとにした + N
meaning: Based on / drawing from — marks the source material or foundation
explanation: をもとに marks what something is based on, derived from, or drawn from. Used when creating, designing, or deciding something using existing information as a foundation. をもとにした modifies a following noun.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"実際の事件をもとにした映画です。","reading":"じっさいのじけんをもとにしたえいがです。","english":"It's a film based on a real incident."},{"japanese":"アンケート結果をもとに、計画を立てました。","reading":"アンケートけっかをもとに、けいかくをたてました。","english":"We drew up a plan based on the survey results."},{"japanese":"データをもとにして分析します。","reading":"データをもとにしてぶんせきします。","english":"I will analyse based on the data."}]

title: 〜わけにはいかない (can't / must not — social/moral constraint)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + わけにはいかない / V-ない + わけにはいかない
meaning: Can't bring myself to / can't afford to — socially or morally can't do (or not do)
explanation: わけにはいかない expresses that despite wanting to, social norms, obligations, or consequences make it impossible. 〜ないわけにはいかない = can't avoid doing (obligated). The constraint is external/social, not just personal ability.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"上司に嘘をつくわけにはいきません。","reading":"じょうしにうそをつくわけにはいきません。","english":"I can't bring myself to lie to my boss."},{"japanese":"ここで諦めるわけにはいかない。","reading":"ここであきらめるわけにはいかない。","english":"I can't afford to give up here."},{"japanese":"お世話になった人の頼みを断るわけにはいきません。","reading":"おせわになったひとのたのみをことわるわけにはいきません。","english":"I can't bring myself to refuse a request from someone who has helped me."}]

title: 〜上で (after doing / in terms of)
pattern: V-た + 上で / N の + 上で
meaning: After doing / on the basis of — marks a prerequisite action before the main one
explanation: 上で after a past-tense verb means 'after doing X (as a prerequisite), then Y'. After a noun it can mean 'in terms of' or 'regarding'. It implies the first action is completed and its result informs the second.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"よく考えた上で、決断しました。","reading":"よくかんがえたうえで、けつだんしました。","english":"I made my decision after careful consideration."},{"japanese":"内容を確認した上で、サインしてください。","reading":"ないようをかくにんしたうえで、サインしてください。","english":"Please sign after confirming the contents."},{"japanese":"ビジネスの上で大切なのは信頼です。","reading":"ビジネスのうえでたいせつなのはしんらいです。","english":"What is important in terms of business is trust."}]

title: 〜にわたって / にわたる (over / spanning — range)
pattern: N + にわたって / にわたる + N
meaning: Over / spanning / throughout — covers a period of time or range of items
explanation: にわたって expresses that something extends over a range or period. The range can be time (3 years), area, or scope (many topics). にわたる is the attributive form modifying a following noun.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"会議は3時間にわたって続きました。","reading":"かいぎは3じかんにわたってつづきました。","english":"The meeting continued for over 3 hours."},{"japanese":"広い範囲にわたる調査が行われました。","reading":"ひろいはんいにわたるちょうさがおこなわれました。","english":"A survey spanning a wide range was conducted."},{"japanese":"長年にわたって研究を続けてきました。","reading":"ながねんにわたってけんきゅうをつづけてきました。","english":"I have continued research over many years."}]

title: 〜に関わらず (regardless of)
pattern: N / V (plain) + に関わらず / にかかわらず
meaning: Regardless of / irrespective of — outcome is the same no matter the condition
explanation: にかかわらず states that the main clause holds regardless of the condition. It is formal and often appears in official statements, rules, or policies. Can follow nouns or plain-form verbs.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"経験の有無に関わらず、応募できます。","reading":"けいけんのうむにかかわらず、おうぼできます。","english":"You can apply regardless of whether you have experience."},{"japanese":"天気に関わらず、イベントは開催します。","reading":"てんきにかかわらず、イベントはかいさいします。","english":"The event will be held regardless of the weather."},{"japanese":"結果に関わらず、全力を尽くします。","reading":"けっかにかかわらず、ぜんりょくをつくします。","english":"Regardless of the result, I will give my all."}]

title: 〜に反して (contrary to / against)
pattern: N + に反して / に反する + N
meaning: Contrary to / against — the result goes against an expectation, wish, or rule
explanation: に反して expresses that something goes contrary to a norm, expectation, or wish. The preceding noun is often 予想, 期待, 規則, or 意志. に反する is the attributive form.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"予想に反して、試験は簡単でした。","reading":"よそうにはんして、しけんはかんたんでした。","english":"Contrary to expectations, the exam was easy."},{"japanese":"規則に反する行為は禁止されています。","reading":"きそくにはんするこういはきんしされています。","english":"Acts contrary to the rules are prohibited."},{"japanese":"父の意志に反して、芸術の道を選びました。","reading":"ちちのいしにはんして、げいじゅつのみちをえらびました。","english":"Contrary to my father's wishes, I chose the path of art."}]

title: 〜に際して (on the occasion of / when doing — formal)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + に際して / N + に際して
meaning: On the occasion of / when — formal marker for an important occasion or action
explanation: に際して is a formal expression marking an important occasion, ceremony, or significant moment. It is used in speeches, official documents, and formal situations. Similar to にあたって but often for more public events.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"入学に際して、注意事項をご確認ください。","reading":"にゅうがくにさいして、ちゅういじこうをごかくにんください。","english":"Upon enrollment, please confirm the precautions."},{"japanese":"契約を結ぶに際して、内容をよく読んでください。","reading":"けいやくをむすぶにさいして、ないようをよくよんでください。","english":"When signing a contract, please read the contents carefully."},{"japanese":"オリンピック開催に際して、多くの準備が必要でした。","reading":"オリンピックかいさいにさいして、おおくのじゅんびがひつようでした。","english":"Preparations were needed on the occasion of hosting the Olympics."}]

title: 〜に先立って (prior to / in advance of)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + に先立って / N + に先立って
meaning: Prior to / before — marks a preparatory or preceding formal action
explanation: に先立って indicates something done in advance or beforehand as a preparation for what follows. It is formal and often appears in official announcements, ceremonies, or preparations for significant events.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"本日の講演に先立って、ご挨拶申し上げます。","reading":"ほんじつのこうえんにさきだって、ごあいさつもうしあげます。","english":"Prior to today's lecture, allow me to offer a greeting."},{"japanese":"発売に先立って、プレビューイベントを開催します。","reading":"はつばいにさきだって、プレビューイベントをかいさいします。","english":"Prior to the sale, we will hold a preview event."},{"japanese":"工事を始めるに先立って、住民に説明しました。","reading":"こうじをはじめるにさきだって、じゅうみんにせつめいしました。","english":"Prior to starting construction, we explained to the residents."}]

title: 〜をはじめ (starting with / including)
pattern: N + をはじめ / をはじめとして
meaning: Starting with / including — marks the leading example of a group
explanation: をはじめ introduces the most representative or notable member of a group, with others following. をはじめとする/として is the attributive form. It implies there are many more examples beyond the one mentioned.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"東京をはじめ、多くの都市で開催されます。","reading":"とうきょうをはじめ、おおくのとしでかいさいされます。","english":"It will be held in many cities, starting with Tokyo."},{"japanese":"田中先生をはじめとする先生方に感謝します。","reading":"たなかせんせいをはじめとするせんせいがたにかんしゃします。","english":"I am grateful to the teachers, starting with Tanaka-sensei."},{"japanese":"日本語をはじめ、多くの言語が話されています。","reading":"にほんごをはじめ、おおくのげんごがはなされています。","english":"Many languages are spoken, including Japanese."}]

title: 〜をめぐって (surrounding / over the issue of)
pattern: N + をめぐって / をめぐる + N
meaning: Surrounding / over / regarding — marks a contested or debated topic
explanation: をめぐって marks the subject around which a dispute, debate, or competition revolves. It implies multiple perspectives or parties involved. をめぐる is the attributive form.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"その問題をめぐって激しい議論が行われました。","reading":"そのもんだいをめぐってはげしいぎろんがおこなわれました。","english":"Fierce debate was conducted surrounding that issue."},{"japanese":"遺産をめぐる家族の争いが続いています。","reading":"いさんをめぐるかぞくのあらそいがつづいています。","english":"The family dispute over the inheritance continues."},{"japanese":"政策をめぐって、賛否両論があります。","reading":"せいさくをめぐって、さんぴりょうろんがあります。","english":"There are opinions both for and against the policy."}]

title: 〜を除いて (excluding / except for)
pattern: N + を除いて / を除く + N
meaning: Excluding / except for — marks what is left out of a group or statement
explanation: を除いて marks an exception. It is formal and more precise than 〜以外. を除く is the attributive form. Often used in official contexts, lists, and formal descriptions.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"土日を除いて、毎日営業しています。","reading":"どにちをのぞいて、まいにちえいぎょうしています。","english":"We are open every day except weekends."},{"japanese":"彼を除いて、全員賛成しました。","reading":"かれをのぞいて、ぜんいんさんせいしました。","english":"Everyone except him agreed."},{"japanese":"特別な場合を除いて、変更はできません。","reading":"とくべつなばあいをのぞいて、へんこうはできません。","english":"Changes cannot be made except in special cases."}]

title: 〜次第 (as soon as / depending on — after verb stem)
pattern: V-stem + 次第 (as soon as) / N + 次第 (depending on / up to)
meaning: As soon as / depending on / up to — triggers the next action or marks dependency
explanation: V-stem + 次第 means 'as soon as [action is complete]'. Different from 次第で/次第では (depending on). The verb-stem usage is common in business Japanese for expressing prompt follow-through.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"準備ができ次第、ご連絡します。","reading":"じゅんびができしだい、ごれんらくします。","english":"I will contact you as soon as preparations are complete."},{"japanese":"到着し次第、会議を始めましょう。","reading":"とうちゃくししだい、かいぎをはじめましょう。","english":"Let's begin the meeting as soon as everyone arrives."},{"japanese":"確認でき次第、お知らせします。","reading":"かくにんできしだい、おしらせします。","english":"I will let you know as soon as I can confirm."}]

title: 〜かねない (might / could — negative potential)
pattern: V-stem + かねない
meaning: Might / could possibly (do something bad) — risk of negative outcome
explanation: かねない warns that something undesirable could happen. It implies the speaker sees a real possibility of the negative outcome. Not used for positive outcomes. Contrasts with かねる (reluctance to do).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"このままでは失敗しかねません。","reading":"このままではしっぱいしかねません。","english":"At this rate, there is a risk of failure."},{"japanese":"そんなことを言うと、誤解されかねませんよ。","reading":"そんなことをいうと、ごかいされかねませんよ。","english":"If you say something like that, you might be misunderstood."},{"japanese":"無理をすると、倒れかねない。","reading":"むりをすると、たおれかねない。","english":"If you push yourself too hard, you might collapse."}]

title: 〜かねる (cannot bring myself to / reluctant)
pattern: V-stem + かねる / かねます
meaning: Cannot bring myself to / find it difficult — reluctance or difficulty doing something
explanation: かねる expresses that the speaker finds it difficult, inappropriate, or impossible to do something. It is polite and indirect — often used in business to decline requests without saying no directly. More formal than できない.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"その件については、お答えしかねます。","reading":"そのけんについては、おこたえしかねます。","english":"I am unable to answer regarding that matter."},{"japanese":"ご要望にはお応えしかねる場合があります。","reading":"ごようぼうにはおこたえしかねるばあいがあります。","english":"There may be cases where we cannot accommodate your request."},{"japanese":"一人で決めかねています。","reading":"ひとりできめかねています。","english":"I find it difficult to decide on my own."}]

title: 〜ざるを得ない (cannot help but / have no choice)
pattern: V-ない stem + ざるを得ない (する → せざるを得ない)
meaning: Cannot help but / have no choice but to — compelled by circumstances
explanation: ざるを得ない is a formal expression indicating there is no alternative. The speaker is compelled by external circumstances. する becomes せざるを得ない. More formal and literary than しかたなく or なければならない.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"証拠を見せられて、認めざるを得ませんでした。","reading":"しょうこをみせられて、みとめざるをえませんでした。","english":"Shown the evidence, I had no choice but to admit it."},{"japanese":"予算の関係で、計画を縮小せざるを得ない。","reading":"よさんのかんけいで、けいかくをしゅくしょうせざるをえない。","english":"Due to budget constraints, I have no choice but to scale down the plan."},{"japanese":"状況を考えると、賛成せざるを得ません。","reading":"じょうきょうをかんがえると、さんせいせざるをえません。","english":"Considering the situation, I have no choice but to agree."}]

title: 〜得る / 得ない (can / cannot happen — possibility)
pattern: V-stem + 得る (うる/える) / 得ない (えない)
meaning: Can happen / cannot happen — logical possibility or impossibility
explanation: 得る (sometimes read うる, sometimes える) expresses logical or theoretical possibility. 得ない means it is impossible or cannot occur. More formal than できる. ありえる = it is possible; ありえない = impossible.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"そんなことがあり得るとは思いませんでした。","reading":"そんなことがありえるとはおもいませんでした。","english":"I didn't think such a thing could happen."},{"japanese":"完全に安全とは言い得ません。","reading":"かんぜんにあんぜんとはいいえません。","english":"It cannot be said to be completely safe."},{"japanese":"この計画は実現し得ると思います。","reading":"このけいかくはじつげんしえるとおもいます。","english":"I think this plan can be realised."}]

title: 〜べきだ (should / ought to — moral obligation)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + べきだ / すべきだ
meaning: Should / ought to — strong moral or logical obligation
explanation: べきだ expresses a moral or logical obligation stronger than ほうがいい. It implies the action is the right or correct thing to do. する becomes すべき or するべき. べきではない = should not (strong prohibition).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"約束は守るべきです。","reading":"やくそくはまもるべきです。","english":"One should keep promises."},{"japanese":"もっと早く謝るべきでした。","reading":"もっとはやくあやまるべきでした。","english":"I should have apologised sooner."},{"japanese":"そのような行動はすべきではありません。","reading":"そのようなこうどうはすべきではありません。","english":"You should not take such actions."}]

title: 〜っぽい (seeming like / -ish — casual)
pattern: V-stem / N / Adj-stem + っぽい
meaning: Seeming like / -ish / tending to — casual impression or tendency
explanation: っぽい is a casual suffix meaning 'seeming like' or 'tends to'. It can attach to nouns (子供っぽい = childish), verb stems (飽きっぽい = prone to getting bored), or colour words (白っぽい = whitish). Colloquial and informal.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"この服、ちょっと子供っぽくない？","reading":"このふく、ちょっとこどもっぽくない？","english":"Isn't this outfit a bit childish?"},{"japanese":"彼女は忘れっぽいですね。","reading":"かのじょはわすれっぽいですね。","english":"She tends to be forgetful."},{"japanese":"この壁、少し白っぽい色ですね。","reading":"このかべ、すこししろっぽいいろですね。","english":"This wall has a whitish colour."}]

title: 〜くらいなら (rather than — lesser of two evils)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + くらいなら / ぐらいなら
meaning: Rather than / if it comes to that — prefers something else over the stated option
explanation: くらいなら presents a choice where the speaker finds the stated option so bad they would prefer even a worse-seeming alternative. It expresses a strong aversion to the first option.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"あんな人に頼むくらいなら、一人でやります。","reading":"あんなひとにたのむくらいなら、ひとりでやります。","english":"Rather than asking that person, I'd rather do it alone."},{"japanese":"負けるくらいなら、最初からやらない。","reading":"まけるくらいなら、さいしょからやらない。","english":"Rather than losing, I wouldn't start in the first place."},{"japanese":"そんな仕事を続けるくらいなら、辞めたほうがいい。","reading":"そんなしごとをつづけるくらいなら、やめたほうがいい。","english":"Rather than continuing that kind of work, you'd better quit."}]

title: 〜てまで (even going so far as to)
pattern: V-て + まで
meaning: Even to the point of doing — emphasises an extreme or undesirable means
explanation: てまで emphasises that the speaker or someone went to an extreme or undesirable length to achieve something. It often implies the action is excessive or the speaker disapproves. Compare with てでも (willingness to use extreme means).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"嘘をついてまで、勝ちたくない。","reading":"うそをついてまで、かちたくない。","english":"I don't want to win even if it means lying."},{"japanese":"そこまでしてまで、続ける必要はあるの？","reading":"そこまでしてまで、つづけるひつようはあるの？","english":"Is there really a need to continue even going that far?"},{"japanese":"無理してまで参加しなくていいです。","reading":"むりしてまでさんかしなくていいです。","english":"You don't need to participate even if it means pushing yourself."}]

title: 〜だけあって (as expected of / no wonder)
pattern: N / V (plain) + だけあって
meaning: As expected of / no wonder — justifies an observation given the known quality
explanation: だけあって expresses that a result is expected or unsurprising given a known quality or fact. It carries an admiring or confirming tone: 'given that X is true, it makes sense that Y'.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"プロだけあって、演奏が素晴らしかった。","reading":"プロだけあって、えんそうがすばらしかった。","english":"As expected of a professional, the performance was wonderful."},{"japanese":"10年間練習しただけあって、上手ですね。","reading":"10ねんかんれんしゅうしただけあって、じょうずですね。","english":"No wonder you're good — you've practiced for 10 years."},{"japanese":"高いだけあって、品質がいいですね。","reading":"たかいだけあって、ひんしつがいいですね。","english":"No wonder the quality is good — it is expensive."}]

title: 〜だけに (precisely because / all the more)
pattern: N / V (plain) + だけに
meaning: Precisely because / all the more — the result is amplified by the quality or circumstance
explanation: だけに means 'because of exactly that quality, the result is even more so'. It highlights that a result (often negative or unfortunate) is especially poignant given the circumstances. Similar to だけあって but often with a heavier emotional weight.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"長年の夢だっただけに、失敗してとても残念でした。","reading":"ながねんのゆめだっただけに、しっぱいしてとてもざんねんでした。","english":"Precisely because it was a long-held dream, the failure was all the more disappointing."},{"japanese":"努力しただけに、結果が出なくて悔しかった。","reading":"どりょくしただけに、けっかがでなくてくやしかった。","english":"Because of all the effort put in, not getting results was frustrating."},{"japanese":"期待が大きかっただけに、失望も大きかった。","reading":"きたいがおおきかっただけに、しつぼうもおおきかった。","english":"The greater the expectation, the greater the disappointment."}]

title: 〜ながら (although / despite — concessive)
pattern: V-stem / N + ながら
meaning: Although / while (concessive) — acknowledges a fact but goes against expectation
explanation: This concessive ながら (distinct from the simultaneous-action ながら) means 'although' or 'despite'. It is used in patterns like 残念ながら (unfortunately) and 知りながら (even though knowing). More formal than が or けど.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"残念ながら、ご要望にお応えできません。","reading":"ざんねんながら、ごようぼうにおこたえできません。","english":"Unfortunately, we cannot accommodate your request."},{"japanese":"知りながら、何も言わなかった。","reading":"しりながら、なにもいわなかった。","english":"Despite knowing, he said nothing."},{"japanese":"小さいながら、とても使いやすいです。","reading":"ちいさいながら、とてもつかいやすいです。","english":"Although small, it is very easy to use."}]

title: 〜つつ (while / although — formal simultaneous or concessive)
pattern: V-stem + つつ / つつも
meaning: While (simultaneous) / although (concessive) — formal literary version of ながら
explanation: つつ is the formal/literary equivalent of ながら. つつも adds a concessive nuance ('even while / although'). Not used in casual conversation. Common in writing, formal speeches, and classical-influenced prose.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"音楽を聴きつつ、仕事をしています。","reading":"おんがくをききつつ、しごとをしています。","english":"I am working while listening to music."},{"japanese":"悪いと知りつつも、やめられません。","reading":"わるいとしりつつも、やめられません。","english":"Even while knowing it's bad, I can't stop."},{"japanese":"時代が変わりつつある。","reading":"じだいがかわりつつある。","english":"The era is in the process of changing."}]

title: 〜つつある (in the process of / gradually)
pattern: V-stem + つつある
meaning: In the process of / gradually — marks ongoing change or progression
explanation: つつある marks a process currently underway, often describing gradual social, environmental, or personal change. It is formal and commonly found in written Japanese, news, and academic writing.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"経済が回復しつつあります。","reading":"けいざいがかいふくしつつあります。","english":"The economy is in the process of recovering."},{"japanese":"彼の体調は改善しつつある。","reading":"かれのたいちょうはかいぜんしつつある。","english":"His physical condition is gradually improving."},{"japanese":"日本の少子化は深刻化しつつあります。","reading":"にほんのしょうしかはしんこくかしつつあります。","english":"Japan's declining birth rate is becoming increasingly serious."}]

title: 〜にあたって (when / on the occasion of — careful action)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + にあたって / N + にあたって
meaning: When / on the occasion of — marks a significant moment requiring care or preparation
explanation: にあたって is similar to に際して but slightly less ceremonial. It marks an important occasion or action, implying care, consideration, or preparation. Common in speeches, official texts, and formal writing.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"新しいプロジェクトを始めるにあたって、計画を立てました。","reading":"あたらしいプロジェクトをはじめるにあたって、けいかくをたてました。","english":"When starting the new project, I made a plan."},{"japanese":"卒業にあたって、先生に感謝の言葉を述べました。","reading":"そつぎょうにあたって、せんせいにかんしゃのことばをのべました。","english":"On the occasion of graduation, I expressed my gratitude to the teacher."},{"japanese":"この仕事を引き受けるにあたって、条件があります。","reading":"このしごとをひきうけるにあたって、じょうけんがあります。","english":"When accepting this work, there are conditions."}]

title: 〜を中心に (centred on / focusing on)
pattern: N + を中心に / を中心として
meaning: Centred on / focused on — marks the central element around which things revolve
explanation: を中心に marks the focal point of activity, attention, or organisation. を中心にした or を中心とする is the attributive form. Common in describing projects, initiatives, geographic arrangements, and group activities.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"東京を中心に、全国で講演を行います。","reading":"とうきょうをちゅうしんに、ぜんこくでこうえんをおこないます。","english":"We will hold lectures nationwide, centred on Tokyo."},{"japanese":"若者を中心に、スマートフォンの利用が増えています。","reading":"わかものをちゅうしんに、スマートフォンのりようがふえています。","english":"Centred on young people, smartphone usage is increasing."},{"japanese":"彼を中心としたチームが結成されました。","reading":"かれをちゅうしんとしたチームがけっせいされました。","english":"A team centred around him was formed."}]

title: 〜に過ぎない (nothing more than / merely)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + に過ぎない / N + に過ぎない
meaning: Nothing more than / merely — downplays or limits the significance of something
explanation: に過ぎない states that something is 'no more than' a certain thing, often downplaying exaggeration or limiting scope. It implies the speaker is correcting an overestimation or providing a humble qualification.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"これは私の個人的な意見に過ぎません。","reading":"これはわたしのこじんてきないけんにすぎません。","english":"This is nothing more than my personal opinion."},{"japanese":"それは偶然に過ぎないと思います。","reading":"それはぐうぜんにすぎないとおもいます。","english":"I think that's merely a coincidence."},{"japanese":"私はただのサラリーマンに過ぎない。","reading":"わたしはただのサラリーマンにすぎない。","english":"I am nothing more than an ordinary office worker."}]

title: 〜にほかならない (none other than / nothing but)
pattern: N / V (plain) + にほかならない
meaning: None other than / is precisely — strong formal assertion of identity or cause
explanation: にほかならない is a formal, emphatic expression meaning 'is precisely X and nothing else'. It identifies the true nature or cause of something definitively. More formal and emphatic than だ alone.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"これは皆さんの努力の結果にほかならない。","reading":"これはみなさんのどりょくのけっかにほかならない。","english":"This is none other than the result of everyone's efforts."},{"japanese":"彼が成功したのは才能があるからにほかならない。","reading":"かれがせいこうしたのはさいのうがあるからにほかならない。","english":"The reason he succeeded is none other than his talent."},{"japanese":"それは愛情にほかならないと思います。","reading":"それはあいじょうにほかならないとおもいます。","english":"That is nothing other than love, I believe."}]

title: 〜というものだ (that's what ... is / it's only natural)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + というものだ
meaning: That's what X is / it's only natural — states a general truth or norm assertively
explanation: というものだ asserts a general truth or social norm. It often carries a tone of 'that's the nature of things' or 'that's exactly what something is'. More emphatic than ものだ alone.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"苦労してこそ、本当の喜びというものだ。","reading":"くろうしてこそ、ほんとうのよろこびというものだ。","english":"True joy is something that comes through hardship."},{"japanese":"親ならば子供を守るというものだ。","reading":"おやならばこどもをまもるというものだ。","english":"It's only natural for a parent to protect their child."},{"japanese":"失敗から学ぶというものですよ。","reading":"しっぱいからまなぶというものですよ。","english":"That's what learning from failure is all about."}]

title: 〜といったN (... type things / things like)
pattern: 「list」 + といった + N
meaning: Such ... as / things like — lists examples of the type of noun that follows
explanation: といった precedes a noun to list examples of that category. Similar to というような but slightly more formal. Common in written Japanese, academic texts, and formal speech.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"経済や政治といった問題について話しました。","reading":"けいざいやせいじといったもんだいについてはなしました。","english":"We talked about issues such as economics and politics."},{"japanese":"サッカーや野球といったスポーツが好きです。","reading":"サッカーややきゅうといったスポーツがすきです。","english":"I like sports such as football and baseball."},{"japanese":"環境といった問題に取り組む必要があります。","reading":"かんきょうといったもんだいにとりくむひつようがあります。","english":"We need to tackle issues like environmental problems."}]

title: 〜ないわけにはいかない (cannot avoid — obligated to do)
pattern: V-ない + わけにはいかない
meaning: Cannot avoid doing / must do — obligated by social or moral reason
explanation: ないわけにはいかない is the negative form: you cannot avoid doing it. The social or moral situation compels the action. It implies reluctance but inevitability. Distinguish from ないわけがない (there's no way it's not true).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"頼まれたのだから、断らないわけにはいきません。","reading":"たのまれたのだから、ことわらないわけにはいきません。","english":"Since I was asked, I cannot avoid not refusing (= I must accept)."},{"japanese":"事実を知ったなら、報告しないわけにはいかない。","reading":"じじつをしったなら、ほうこくしないわけにはいかない。","english":"Now that I know the facts, I cannot avoid reporting them."},{"japanese":"お世話になった方の結婚式には出席しないわけにはいかない。","reading":"おせわになったかたのけっこんしきにはしゅっせきしないわけにはいかない。","english":"I cannot avoid attending the wedding of someone who has looked after me."}]

title: 〜に基づいて (based on / in accordance with)
pattern: N + に基づいて / に基づく + N
meaning: Based on / in accordance with — marks the foundation or evidence for an action
explanation: に基づいて expresses that an action or decision is based on a specific foundation (rules, data, evidence, law). に基づく is the attributive form. More formal and precise than をもとに.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"データに基づいて分析しました。","reading":"データにもとづいてぶんせきしました。","english":"I analysed based on the data."},{"japanese":"法律に基づいて処罰されます。","reading":"ほうりつにもとづいてしょばつされます。","english":"Punishment will be carried out in accordance with the law."},{"japanese":"事実に基づく報告書を提出してください。","reading":"じじつにもとづくほうこくしょをていしゅつしてください。","english":"Please submit a report based on the facts."}]

title: 〜に照らして (in light of / based on)
pattern: N + に照らして / に照らし合わせて
meaning: In light of / judged against — evaluating by comparing to a standard or criterion
explanation: に照らして means to evaluate or judge something by holding it up against a standard, rule, or reference. に照らし合わせて is a stronger form emphasising direct comparison. Common in legal, academic, and evaluative contexts.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"規則に照らして考えると、これは問題です。","reading":"きそくにてらしてかんがえると、これはもんだいです。","english":"In light of the rules, this is a problem."},{"japanese":"過去の事例に照らして判断しました。","reading":"かこのじれいにてらしてはんだんしました。","english":"I made a judgement in light of past cases."},{"japanese":"現実に照らし合わせて、計画を修正します。","reading":"げんじつにてらしあわせて、けいかくをしゅうせいします。","english":"I will revise the plan by comparing it against reality."}]

title: 〜にしても〜にしても (whether ... or)
pattern: N/V + にしても + N/V + にしても
meaning: Whether ... or — the result holds in both cases
explanation: にしても〜にしても states that regardless of which of two options applies, the result or conclusion is the same. It covers both cases to show the conclusion is unavoidable.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"賛成にしても反対にしても、意見を述べてください。","reading":"さんせいにしてもはんたいにしても、いけんをのべてください。","english":"Whether you agree or disagree, please state your opinion."},{"japanese":"行くにしても行かないにしても、早めに決めてください。","reading":"いくにしてもいかないにしても、はやめにきめてください。","english":"Whether you go or not, please decide early."},{"japanese":"成功にしても失敗にしても、挑戦することに意味があります。","reading":"せいこうにしてもしっぱいにしても、ちょうせんすることにいみがあります。","english":"Whether you succeed or fail, there is meaning in trying."}]

title: 〜に限らず (not limited to / not only)
pattern: N + に限らず
meaning: Not limited to / not only — broadens the scope beyond the stated example
explanation: に限らず means 'not limited to / not only X'. It extends the statement to include other things beyond the one mentioned. Formal, often found in written Japanese.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"日本に限らず、世界中で問題になっています。","reading":"にほんにかぎらず、せかいじゅうでもんだいになっています。","english":"Not limited to Japan, it has become a problem worldwide."},{"japanese":"大人に限らず、子供も楽しめるイベントです。","reading":"おとなにかぎらず、こどももたのしめるイベントです。","english":"It's an event not limited to adults — children can enjoy it too."},{"japanese":"女性に限らず、男性も参加できます。","reading":"じょせいにかぎらず、だんせいもさんかできます。","english":"Not limited to women, men can also participate."}]

title: 〜ことなく (without doing — formal)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + ことなく
meaning: Without doing — formal version of ずに/ないで
explanation: ことなく is a formal literary expression meaning 'without doing'. It is more formal than ずに and found in written Japanese, formal speech, and literary contexts. Often appears in set phrases expressing continuous effort.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"諦めることなく、最後まで頑張りました。","reading":"あきらめることなく、さいごまでがんばりました。","english":"Without giving up, I persevered to the end."},{"japanese":"休むことなく、24時間働き続けました。","reading":"やすむことなく、24じかんはたらきつづけました。","english":"Without resting, I continued working for 24 hours."},{"japanese":"変わることなく、ずっとそこにある。","reading":"かわることなく、ずっとそこにある。","english":"Without changing, it has always been there."}]

title: 〜ことに (to my ... / the fact is — emotional emphasis)
pattern: V-た / Adj (plain) + ことに
meaning: To my [emotion] / the fact is — marks an emotional reaction to a fact
explanation: ことに follows an adjective or past verb to express the speaker's emotional response to a situation. Common patterns: 残念なことに (regrettably), 嬉しいことに (happily), 驚いたことに (surprisingly).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"残念なことに、彼は来られなかった。","reading":"ざんねんなことに、かれはこられなかった。","english":"Regrettably, he was unable to come."},{"japanese":"嬉しいことに、試験に合格しました。","reading":"うれしいことに、しけんにごうかくしました。","english":"Happily, I passed the exam."},{"japanese":"驚いたことに、彼女はすでに知っていた。","reading":"おどろいたことに、かのじょはすでにしっていた。","english":"Surprisingly, she already knew."}]

title: 〜に沿って (along / in line with)
pattern: N + に沿って / に沿った + N
meaning: Along / in line with / following — proceeds parallel to or in accordance with
explanation: に沿って means to go along a physical path or to follow a plan, policy, or wish. に沿った is the attributive form. More concrete (along a river) or figurative (along the plan) uses are both common.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"川に沿って歩きました。","reading":"かわにそってあるきました。","english":"I walked along the river."},{"japanese":"計画に沿って進めましょう。","reading":"けいかくにそってすすめましょう。","english":"Let's proceed in line with the plan."},{"japanese":"方針に沿った対応が求められます。","reading":"ほうしんにそったたいおうがもとめられます。","english":"A response in line with the policy is required."}]

title: 〜からすると / からすれば (from the perspective of / judging from)
pattern: N + からすると / からすれば / からして
meaning: From the perspective of / judging from — marks the basis for a judgement or inference
explanation: からすると/からすれば marks the standpoint from which a judgement is made. It is used when inferring or evaluating based on available information or someone's perspective. からして can stress the starting point ('even from just X...').
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"外見からすると、彼は若く見えます。","reading":"がいけんからすると、かれはわかくみえます。","english":"Judging from his appearance, he looks young."},{"japanese":"専門家の立場からすれば、この方法は危険です。","reading":"せんもんかのたちばからすれば、このほうほうはきけんです。","english":"From the perspective of an expert, this method is dangerous."},{"japanese":"声からして、彼女だとわかった。","reading":"こえからして、かのじょだとわかった。","english":"Just from her voice, I knew it was her."}]

title: 〜てでも (even by doing — determination)
pattern: V-て + でも
meaning: Even by doing — willingness to use extreme or undesirable means
explanation: てでも expresses strong determination to achieve a goal, even if the means are difficult or undesirable. The speaker declares they will do whatever it takes. Contrasts with てまで (which expresses reluctance).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"借金をしてでも、この家を買いたい。","reading":"しゃっきんをしてでも、このいえをかいたい。","english":"Even if I have to borrow money, I want to buy this house."},{"japanese":"徹夜してでも、この仕事を終わらせます。","reading":"てつやしてでも、このしごとをおわらせます。","english":"Even if I have to pull an all-nighter, I'll finish this work."},{"japanese":"どんな手を使ってでも、勝ちに行く。","reading":"どんなてをつかってでも、かちにいく。","english":"I'll go for the win using whatever means it takes."}]

title: 〜にしたって / にしても (even for / even if — extended scope)
pattern: N + にしたって / にしても
meaning: Even for / even if — broadens a statement to include an additional case
explanation: にしても and にしたって (casual) introduce an additional case that also applies, or acknowledge a concession while maintaining the main point. Similar to even if / even for. にしたって is slightly more colloquial.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼にしたって、問題を知っていたはずだ。","reading":"かれにしたって、もんだいをしっていたはずだ。","english":"Even for him, he must have known about the problem."},{"japanese":"忙しいにしても、連絡くらいすべきでした。","reading":"いそがしいにしても、れんらくくらいすべきでした。","english":"Even if you were busy, you should have at least contacted me."},{"japanese":"子供にしても、それくらいは分かります。","reading":"こどもにしても、それくらいはわかります。","english":"Even a child would understand that much."}]

title: 〜を契機に (taking ... as an opportunity / triggered by)
pattern: N + を契機に / を契機として
meaning: Taking ... as an opportunity / triggered by — marks a turning point event
explanation: を契機に marks an event that served as a trigger or turning point for a change. It implies the change was significant and the event that caused it was a clear catalyst.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"その失敗を契機に、生活を見直しました。","reading":"そのしっぱいをけいきに、せいかつをみなおしました。","english":"Taking that failure as an opportunity, I reassessed my life."},{"japanese":"この事件を契機として、法律が改正されました。","reading":"このじけんをけいきとして、ほうりつがかいせいされました。","english":"Triggered by this incident, the law was revised."},{"japanese":"留学を契機に、将来の方向が変わりました。","reading":"りゅうがくをけいきに、しょうらいのほうこうがかわりました。","english":"Studying abroad served as a turning point that changed my future direction."}]

title: 〜を踏まえて (based on / taking ... into account)
pattern: N + を踏まえて / を踏まえた + N
meaning: Based on / taking into account — acts with awareness of existing facts or context
explanation: を踏まえて means to proceed based on or taking into account relevant existing information, results, or context. It implies the information has been properly considered before acting.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"前回の結果を踏まえて、計画を修正しました。","reading":"ぜんかいのけっかをふまえて、けいかくをしゅうせいしました。","english":"Based on last time's results, I revised the plan."},{"japanese":"現状を踏まえた上で、対策を考えましょう。","reading":"げんじょうをふまえたうえで、たいさくをかんがえましょう。","english":"Let's consider countermeasures based on the current situation."},{"japanese":"ご意見を踏まえて対応します。","reading":"ごいけんをふまえてたいおうします。","english":"I will respond taking your opinion into account."}]

title: 〜に鑑みて (in view of / considering — very formal)
pattern: N + に鑑みて / に鑑みた + N
meaning: In view of / considering — very formal, used in official documents
explanation: に鑑みて is a very formal expression meaning to consider or evaluate something in light of a standard, precedent, or important fact. Found in legal documents, official speeches, and formal writing. Rarely used in daily conversation.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"昨今の状況に鑑みて、方針を変更します。","reading":"さっこんのじょうきょうにかんがみて、ほうしんをへんこうします。","english":"In view of the current situation, we are changing our policy."},{"japanese":"過去の実績に鑑みて、彼を選びました。","reading":"かこのじっせきにかんがみて、かれをえらびました。","english":"In view of past results, we chose him."},{"japanese":"社会情勢に鑑みた対応が必要です。","reading":"しゃかいじょうせいにかんがみたたいおうがひつようです。","english":"A response in view of the social situation is necessary."}]

title: 〜かといえば / かというと (if I were to say / speaking of)
pattern: 疑問詞 + かといえば / かというと
meaning: If asked whether / if I were to say — introduces an explanation or qualification
explanation: 〜かといえば/かというと introduces an answer or clarification to the question implied. Often used to partially agree then add nuance: 好きかといえば、そうでもない = if you ask if I like it, not really. Common in explanatory or reflective speech.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"なぜそうしたかといえば、心配だったからです。","reading":"なぜそうしたかといえば、しんぱいだったからです。","english":"If you ask why I did that, it's because I was worried."},{"japanese":"うまいかというと、そうでもないですね。","reading":"うまいかというと、そうでもないですね。","english":"If you ask whether it's good, not particularly."},{"japanese":"好きかといえば、まあそうでもないかな。","reading":"すきかといえば、まあそうでもないかな。","english":"If asked if I like it, well, not really I suppose."}]

title: 〜というより (rather than / more like)
pattern: S + というより
meaning: Rather than / more accurately — corrects or refines what was just said
explanation: というより replaces or refines the preceding description with a more accurate one. The speaker says X, then corrects or clarifies: 'rather, it's Y'. Used when a more precise term exists or to soften an assertion.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼は怒っているというより、悲しんでいる。","reading":"かれはおこっているというより、かなしんでいる。","english":"Rather than angry, he seems sad."},{"japanese":"これは便利というより、必要不可欠です。","reading":"これはべんりというより、ひつようふかけつです。","english":"Rather than convenient, this is indispensable."},{"japanese":"疲れているというより、眠いんです。","reading":"つかれているというより、ねむいんです。","english":"Rather than tired, I'm actually sleepy."}]

title: 〜に反する (to go against — attributive)
pattern: N + に反する + N
meaning: That goes against / contrary to — attributive form modifying a noun
explanation: に反する is the attributive (noun-modifying) form of に反して. Used when modifying a noun: 規則に反する行為 = conduct contrary to the rules. Common in legal, ethical, and formal contexts.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"規則に反する行為は許されません。","reading":"きそくにはんするこういはゆるされません。","english":"Conduct contrary to the rules is not permitted."},{"japanese":"命令に反する行動を取ってしまいました。","reading":"めいれいにはんするこうどうをとってしまいました。","english":"I ended up taking action contrary to orders."},{"japanese":"常識に反する判断に驚きました。","reading":"じょうしきにはんするはんだんにおどろきました。","english":"I was surprised by the judgement contrary to common sense."}]

title: 〜ものがある (there is a sense of / something about it)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + ものがある
meaning: There is a certain quality / something about it — expresses an indescribable sense
explanation: ものがある expresses that there is some inherent quality or feeling about something that is hard to fully articulate. It is used when the speaker perceives a notable or strong quality. More formal than 感じがする.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"彼の演奏には感動するものがある。","reading":"かれのえんそうにはかんどうするものがある。","english":"There is something moving about his performance."},{"japanese":"この問題には考えさせられるものがある。","reading":"このもんだいにはかんがえさせられるものがある。","english":"There is something thought-provoking about this problem."},{"japanese":"彼女の言葉には人を引きつけるものがある。","reading":"かのじょのことばにはひとをひきつけるものがある。","english":"There is something attractive about her words."}]

title: 〜ようがない / ようもない (no way to / cannot possibly)
pattern: V-stem + ようがない / ようもない
meaning: There is no way to / impossible to — complete impossibility of method
explanation: ようがない expresses that there is absolutely no means or way to do something. Unlike できない (lack of ability), ようがない emphasises the absence of any possible method at all.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"あの人の言っていることは、理解しようがない。","reading":"あのひとのいっていることは、りかいしようがない。","english":"There's no way to understand what that person is saying."},{"japanese":"連絡先も知らないから、知らせようがない。","reading":"れんらくさきもしらないから、しらせようがない。","english":"I don't even know their contact information, so there's no way to notify them."},{"japanese":"証拠がないと、証明しようがありません。","reading":"しょうこがないと、しょうめいしようがありません。","english":"Without evidence, there's no way to prove it."}]

title: 〜に足りない / 足る (worth doing / not worth)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + に足る / に足りない
meaning: Worth doing / not worth — something merits or does not merit the action
explanation: に足る/に足りる means 'worthy of / sufficient for'. に足らない/足りない means 'not worth / not sufficient'. Formal and literary expressions used in evaluative contexts.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"信頼するに足る人物です。","reading":"しんらいするにたるじんぶつです。","english":"He is a person worthy of trust."},{"japanese":"そんなことは相手にするに足りない。","reading":"そんなことはあいてにするにたりない。","english":"That's not worth dealing with."},{"japanese":"彼の意見は検討するに値します。","reading":"かれのいけんはけんとうするにあたいします。","english":"His opinion is worth considering."}]

title: 〜を皮切りに (beginning with / starting with — first in a series)
pattern: N + を皮切りに / を皮切りとして
meaning: Beginning with / starting with — marks the first event in a series
explanation: を皮切りに marks the opening event that starts a series of similar events. It implies subsequent events will follow. より formal than から, and carries a sense of momentum or occasion.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"東京公演を皮切りに、全国ツアーが始まります。","reading":"とうきょうこうえんをかわきりに、ぜんこくツアーがはじまります。","english":"Starting with the Tokyo performance, a nationwide tour begins."},{"japanese":"この商品の発売を皮切りに、新展開が続きます。","reading":"このしょうひんのはつばいをかわきりに、しんてんかいがつづきます。","english":"Beginning with this product launch, new developments will follow."},{"japanese":"彼女の一言を皮切りとして、議論が始まった。","reading":"かのじょのひとことをかわきりとして、ぎろんがはじまった。","english":"Starting with her single remark, a debate began."}]

title: 〜といえども (even though / even if — formal concession)
pattern: N / V (plain) + といえども
meaning: Even though / even if — formal concessive acknowledging a fact while contrasting
explanation: といえども is a formal/literary concessive meaning 'even though / even if'. It acknowledges a fact or quality but then presents a contrasting outcome. More elevated in register than ても or のに.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"ベテランといえども、失敗することはある。","reading":"ベテランといえども、しっぱいすることはある。","english":"Even a veteran can make mistakes."},{"japanese":"子供といえども、罰せられることがある。","reading":"こどもといえども、ばっせられることがある。","english":"Even a child can be punished."},{"japanese":"冗談といえども、傷つく言葉はよくない。","reading":"じょうだんといえども、きずつくことばはよくない。","english":"Even as a joke, hurtful words are not good."}]

title: 〜をもって (with / by means of — formal)
pattern: N + をもって
meaning: With / by means of / as of — formal marker for means, reason, or time limit
explanation: をもって is a formal expression with two main uses: (1) by means of / with (tool or quality); (2) as of [date/time] — often used in formal announcements: 本日をもって. More formal than で.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"本日をもって、受付を終了いたします。","reading":"ほんじつをもって、うけつけをしゅうりょういたします。","english":"As of today, we will close the reception."},{"japanese":"誠意をもって対応いたします。","reading":"せいいをもってたいおうしたします。","english":"We will respond with sincerity."},{"japanese":"努力をもって、困難を乗り越えました。","reading":"どりょくをもって、こんなんをのりこえました。","english":"With effort, I overcame the difficulty."}]

title: 〜いかんによって / いかんでは (depending on — formal)
pattern: N + のいかんによって / いかんでは
meaning: Depending on — formal expression for outcome contingent on a factor
explanation: いかん is a formal/written word meaning 'the state of / what it is like'. のいかんによって/いかんでは expresses that the outcome depends on how things turn out or what the situation is. Formal and bureaucratic in register.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"結果のいかんによっては、計画を変更します。","reading":"けっかのいかんによっては、けいかくをへんこうします。","english":"Depending on the results, we may change the plan."},{"japanese":"経緯のいかんによって、対応が異なります。","reading":"けいいのいかんによって、たいおうがことなります。","english":"The response differs depending on the circumstances."},{"japanese":"態度のいかんでは、入学を拒否することがあります。","reading":"たいどのいかんでは、にゅうがくをきょひすることがあります。","english":"Depending on the attitude, admission may be refused."}]

title: 〜とは (definition / surprise — formal clause marker)
pattern: N / S (plain) + とは
meaning: As for / what is called — defines, explains, or expresses surprise about something
explanation: とは introduces a definition or explanation (〜とは〜のことです) or expresses surprise/disbelief when something unexpected occurs (まさかこんなことになるとは！). Formal; casual version is って or というのは.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"自由とは、責任を伴うものです。","reading":"じゆうとは、せきにんをともなうものです。","english":"Freedom is something that comes with responsibility."},{"japanese":"まさかここで会うとは思いませんでした。","reading":"まさかここであうとはおもいませんでした。","english":"I never thought I would meet you here."},{"japanese":"信頼とはどういうものか、考えさせられました。","reading":"しんらいとはどういうものか、かんがえさせられました。","english":"I was made to think about what trust really is."}]

title: 〜として扱う / 〜として見る (treat as / see as)
pattern: N + として扱う / として見る / として認める
meaning: Treat as / regard as / recognise as — applies a category or status to something
explanation: として followed by action verbs like 扱う (treat), 見る (see/view), 認める (recognise) expresses how something is classified or treated. It is the evaluative use of として, applied in formal contexts.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"この件は緊急事態として扱います。","reading":"このけんはきんきゅうじたいとしてあつかいます。","english":"We will treat this matter as an emergency."},{"japanese":"彼の行動は問題として見ています。","reading":"かれのこうどうはもんだいとしてみています。","english":"We view his actions as a problem."},{"japanese":"このシステムを正式なものとして認めました。","reading":"このシステムをせいしきなものとしてみとめました。","english":"We have recognised this system as an official one."}]

title: 〜からといって (just because ... doesn't mean)
pattern: S (plain) + からといって + negative or limiting conclusion
meaning: Just because ... doesn't mean — dismisses a naive inference from a given fact
explanation: からといって warns against drawing an automatic conclusion from a premise. It says 'just because A, it doesn't follow that B'. The main clause typically contains a negative or limiting statement.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"お金があるからといって、幸せとは限りません。","reading":"おかねがあるからといって、しあわせとはかぎりません。","english":"Just because you have money doesn't mean you're happy."},{"japanese":"知っているからといって、何でも言っていいわけじゃない。","reading":"しっているからといって、なんでもいっていいわけじゃない。","english":"Just because you know something doesn't mean you can say anything."},{"japanese":"有名だからといって、いい映画とは限りません。","reading":"ゆうめいだからといって、いいえいがとはかぎりません。","english":"Just because it's famous doesn't mean it's a good film."}]

title: 〜に相違ない (without doubt — very formal)
pattern: S (plain) + に相違ない
meaning: Without doubt / certainly — formal equivalent of に違いない
explanation: に相違ない is the formal/written equivalent of に違いない. It expresses certainty or strong conviction. Used in formal writing, legal contexts, and official documents rather than spoken Japanese.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"これは本物に相違ない。","reading":"これはほんものにそういない。","english":"This is without doubt the real thing."},{"japanese":"彼が犯人に相違ないと確信しています。","reading":"かれがはんにんにそういないとかくしんしています。","english":"I am convinced that he is without doubt the culprit."},{"japanese":"その事実は誤りに相違ありません。","reading":"そのじじつはあやまりにそういありません。","english":"That fact is without doubt an error."}]

title: 〜向きだ / 向きの (suitable for / facing)
pattern: N + 向きだ / 向きの + N
meaning: Suitable for / oriented toward — matches or is appropriate for the target
explanation: 向きだ indicates suitability or orientation toward something. 〜向きの modifies a noun. Distinct from 向けに (aimed at / intended for which marks design intent) — 向きだ marks natural suitability or fit.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"この仕事は彼女向きだと思います。","reading":"このしごとはかのじょむきだとおもいます。","english":"I think this job suits her."},{"japanese":"これは初心者向きの参考書です。","reading":"これはしょしんしゃむきのさんこうしょです。","english":"This is a reference book suitable for beginners."},{"japanese":"南向きの部屋は日当たりがいいです。","reading":"みなみむきのへやはひあたりがいいです。","english":"A south-facing room gets good sunlight."}]

title: 〜に越したことはない (nothing better than / best to)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + に越したことはない / Adj + に越したことはない
meaning: Nothing is better than / best to — states the ideal option
explanation: に越したことはない means that while not strictly necessary, X is the best course of action. It is often used as advice: the more, the better; the safer, the better. Casual shortening is に越したことない.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"健康は大切にするに越したことはない。","reading":"けんこうはたいせつにするにこしたことはない。","english":"There is nothing better than taking care of your health."},{"japanese":"早めに準備するに越したことはありません。","reading":"はやめにじゅんびするにこしたことはありません。","english":"Nothing is better than preparing early."},{"japanese":"安全に越したことはないですよ。","reading":"あんぜんにこしたことはないですよ。","english":"Nothing is better than being safe."}]

title: 〜てはじめて (only after doing / not until)
pattern: V-て + はじめて
meaning: Only after doing / not until — something becomes true or understood only after the action
explanation: てはじめて expresses that something is only realised, possible, or true after a specific experience or action has occurred. It often carries the nuance of appreciation or understanding gained through experience.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"失って初めて、その大切さがわかります。","reading":"うしなってはじめて、そのたいせつさがわかります。","english":"Only after losing it do you understand its importance."},{"japanese":"実際にやってみて初めて、難しさがわかりました。","reading":"じっさいにやってみてはじめて、むずかしさがわかりました。","english":"Only after actually trying did I understand the difficulty."},{"japanese":"日本に来て初めて、本当の日本語を学べました。","reading":"にほんにきてはじめて、ほんとうのにほんごをまなべました。","english":"Only after coming to Japan was I able to learn real Japanese."}]

title: 〜に際し (on the occasion of — ultra-formal)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + に際し / N + に際し
meaning: On the occasion of — extremely formal, used in official texts and ceremonies
explanation: に際し is the shortened (pre-noun/pre-clause) form of に際して. It is even more formal and often appears in written texts, official proclamations, and formal speeches where に際して would also be appropriate.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"ご入学に際し、心よりお祝い申し上げます。","reading":"ごにゅうがくにさいし、こころよりおいわいもうしあげます。","english":"On the occasion of your enrollment, I offer my sincere congratulations."},{"japanese":"プロジェクト開始に際し、説明会を行います。","reading":"プロジェクトかいしにさいし、せつめいかいをおこないます。","english":"On the occasion of the project start, we will hold a briefing."},{"japanese":"渡航に際し、必要な書類を準備してください。","reading":"とこうにさいし、ひつようなしょるいをじゅんびしてください。","english":"On the occasion of your departure, please prepare the necessary documents."}]

title: 〜を通じて / 通して (through / throughout)
pattern: N + を通じて / を通して
meaning: Through / by way of / throughout — marks a medium, channel, or entire duration
explanation: These two forms are nearly interchangeable. Both mean 'through (a medium)' or 'throughout (a period)'. を通じて has a slightly more formal nuance and can emphasise the mediating function; を通して can be more literal (physically through) or metaphorical.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"スポーツを通じて、友情が育まれます。","reading":"スポーツをつうじて、ゆうじょうがはぐくまれます。","english":"Through sports, friendship is nurtured."},{"japanese":"一年を通して、平均気温は高いです。","reading":"いちねんをとおして、へいきんきおんはたかいです。","english":"Throughout the year, the average temperature is high."},{"japanese":"メールを通じて申し込んでください。","reading":"メールをつうじてもうしこんでください。","english":"Please apply through email."}]

title: 〜にあたる (to correspond to / be equivalent to)
pattern: N / V (plain) + にあたる
meaning: Correspond to / be equivalent to / fall on — marks equivalence or classification
explanation: にあたる expresses that something falls into a certain category, corresponds to something, or falls on a particular date. It can also mean 'be equivalent to' in a conceptual or structural sense.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"今日は創立記念日にあたります。","reading":"きょうはそうりつきねんびにあたります。","english":"Today falls on the founding anniversary."},{"japanese":"この役職は部長にあたります。","reading":"このやくしょくはぶちょうにあたります。","english":"This position corresponds to the department head."},{"japanese":"英語の 'uncle' は日本語で叔父にあたります。","reading":"えいごの 'uncle' はにほんごでおじにあたります。","english":"The English 'uncle' corresponds to 叔父 in Japanese."}]

title: 〜反面 (on the other hand / while)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + 反面 / N の + 反面
meaning: On the other hand / while — presents contrasting aspects of the same subject
explanation: 反面 presents two contrasting sides of the same person, thing, or situation. The same subject has both qualities simultaneously. More formal than が or 一方で, and the contrast is usually presented as a natural trade-off.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"便利な反面、プライバシーの問題があります。","reading":"べんりなはんめん、プライバシーのもんだいがあります。","english":"While convenient, there are privacy issues."},{"japanese":"彼は優しい反面、優柔不断なところがある。","reading":"かれはやさしいはんめん、ゆうじゅうふだんなところがある。","english":"While he is kind, he has an indecisive side."},{"japanese":"都会は便利な反面、自然が少ないです。","reading":"とかいはべんりなはんめん、しぜんがすくないです。","english":"While cities are convenient, there is little nature."}]

title: 〜一方だ (keeps getting more and more)
pattern: V (plain non-past) + 一方だ
meaning: Keeps getting more and more — unidirectional continuous change
explanation: 一方だ indicates a trend that is continuously moving in one direction with no sign of stopping. Usually used for negative trends but can describe positive ones. Distinct from 一方で (on the other hand).
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"物価が上がる一方です。","reading":"ぶっかがあがるいっぽうです。","english":"Prices just keep going up."},{"japanese":"状況が悪化する一方だ。","reading":"じょうきょうがあっかするいっぽうだ。","english":"The situation just keeps getting worse."},{"japanese":"仕事は増える一方で、休めません。","reading":"しごとはふえるいっぽうで、やすめません。","english":"Work just keeps increasing and I can't rest."}]

title: 〜一方で (on the other hand / while)
pattern: V/Adj (plain) + 一方で / N の + 一方で
meaning: On the other hand / while — presents a contrast or two simultaneous aspects
explanation: 一方で presents a contrasting view, a parallel situation, or two things happening at the same time. It can mean 'while doing A, B' or 'on the other hand'. More neutral than 反面 and widely used in spoken and written Japanese.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"仕事をする一方で、趣味も大切にしています。","reading":"しごとをするいっぽうで、しゅみもたいせつにしています。","english":"While working, I also cherish my hobbies."},{"japanese":"科学が進歩する一方で、新たな問題も出てきた。","reading":"かがくがしんぽするいっぽうで、あらたなもんだいもでてきた。","english":"While science advances, new problems have also emerged."},{"japanese":"賛成する人がいる一方で、反対意見もあります。","reading":"さんせいするひとがいるいっぽうで、はんたいいけんもあります。","english":"While some people agree, there are also opposing views."}]

title: 〜ているところを (catching someone in the act of)
pattern: V-ている + ところを
meaning: Catching in the act of — marking the moment someone is caught doing something
explanation: ているところを expresses that someone is caught, seen, or disturbed in the middle of doing something. Often used in passive constructions: 見られた (was seen), つかまった (was caught). The act was in progress when discovered.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"サボっているところを上司に見られてしまいました。","reading":"サボっているところをじょうしにみられてしまいました。","english":"I was caught slacking by my boss."},{"japanese":"寝ているところを起こされました。","reading":"ねているところをおこされました。","english":"I was woken up while I was sleeping."},{"japanese":"食べているところを写真に撮られました。","reading":"たべているところをしゃしんにとられました。","english":"I was photographed in the act of eating."}]

title: 〜を抜きにして (setting aside / without)
pattern: N + を抜きにして / を抜きにしては
meaning: Setting aside / without — removing or leaving out a factor for the purpose of the statement
explanation: を抜きにして means 'setting X aside / without counting X'. を抜きにしては means 'without X it would be impossible (for the following to be true)'. Often used to acknowledge what is being left out.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"冗談を抜きにして、真剣に話しましょう。","reading":"じょうだんをぬきにして、しんけんにはなしましょう。","english":"Setting jokes aside, let's talk seriously."},{"japanese":"皆さんの協力を抜きにしては、成功できませんでした。","reading":"みなさんのきょうりょくをぬきにしては、せいこうできませんでした。","english":"Without everyone's cooperation, we could not have succeeded."},{"japanese":"この問題を抜きにして、進めることはできません。","reading":"このもんだいをぬきにして、すすめることはできません。","english":"We cannot proceed without addressing this issue."}]

title: 〜ぶりに (for the first time in [period])
pattern: Time period + ぶりに
meaning: For the first time in [period] — returning to something after an interval
explanation: 〜ぶりに marks how long it has been since something last occurred. 3年ぶりに = for the first time in 3 years. Can only follow time expressions. Expresses the gap between the previous occurrence and the current one.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"3年ぶりに故郷に帰りました。","reading":"3ねんぶりにこきょうにかえりました。","english":"I returned home for the first time in 3 years."},{"japanese":"久しぶりに会えて嬉しいです。","reading":"ひさしぶりにあえてうれしいです。","english":"I'm happy to see you after such a long time."},{"japanese":"1週間ぶりにゆっくり眠れました。","reading":"1しゅうかんぶりにゆっくりねむれました。","english":"I was able to sleep properly for the first time in a week."}]

title: 〜ないことには (unless / without doing)
pattern: V-ない + ことには
meaning: Unless / without doing — states a necessary condition without which something cannot happen
explanation: ないことには expresses a necessary prerequisite: unless X happens, Y is impossible. It is stronger and more formal than なければ. The main clause is usually negative or expresses impossibility.
examplesJson: [{"japanese":"実際にやってみないことには、わかりません。","reading":"じっさいにやってみないことには、わかりません。","english":"Unless you actually try it, you won't know."},{"japanese":"許可をもらわないことには、始められません。","reading":"きょかをもらわないことには、はじめられません。","english":"Unless we get permission, we cannot start."},{"japanese":"直接話さないことには、解決できません。","reading":"ちょくせつはなさないことには、かいけつできません。","english":"Unless we talk directly, we can't resolve this."}]

title: 〜いかんだ / いかんにかかわらず (depending on / regardless of)
pattern: N + のいかんだ / のいかんにかかわらず
